content.problemStatement:
The beauty of an array AAA of length NNN is defined as follows.

Let SSS be a set of pairs of integers satisfying the following conditions:


Each element (x,y)(x, y)(x,y) of SSS should satisfy 1≤x≤y≤N1 \leq x \leq y \leq N1≤x≤y≤N.
For each (x,y)∈S(x, y) \in S(x,y)∈S, gcd⁡(Ax,Ax+1,Ax+2,…,Ay)=1\gcd(A_x, A_{x+1}, A_{x+2}, \ldots, A_y) = 1gcd(Ax​,Ax+1​,Ax+2​,…,Ay​)=1 should hold.

That is, the GCD of the subarray of AAA from index xxx to index yyy (inclusive) should be 111.
For any two elements (x1,y1),(x2,y2)∈S(x_1, y_1), (x_2, y_2) \in S(x1​,y1​),(x2​,y2​)∈S, either y1<x2y_1 \lt x_2y1​<x2​ or y2<x1y_2 \lt x_1y2​<x1​ should hold.

That is, the segments [x1,y1][x_1, y_1][x1​,y1​] and [x2,y2][x_2, y_2][x2​,y2​] shouldn't intersect.
The beauty of AAA is the maximum size of such a set SSS.

In simpler words, the beauty of AAA is the maximum number of mutually disjoint subarrays of AAA that can be found, each of which has GCD 111.

You are given two positive integers NNN and KKK.

There are KNK^NKN arrays of length NNN whose elements are integers between 111 and KKK. Find the sum of all their beauties.

This value can be large, so compute it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the sum of beauty across all KNK^NKN arrays, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 400400400.
The sum of KKK over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1042\cdot 10^42⋅104.
**Subtask 1 (15 points):** The sum of KKK across all test cases won't exceed 400400400.
**Subtask 2 (25 points):** The sum of KKK across all test cases won't exceed 200020002000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There is only one array [1][1][1], and its beauty is 1.
Test case 222: There are 444 possible arrays, that are:

[1,1][1, 1][1,1], with a beauty of 222.
[1,2][1, 2][1,2], with a beauty of 111.
[2,1][2, 1][2,1], with a beauty of 111.
[2,2][2, 2][2,2], with a beauty of 000.

The sum of all beauties is 444.
